capability_objective: For a read-only operations check, Search separately for “Damaged exhibit shipment” in Museum Desk and “Duplicate membership charge” in Billing Desk, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete case records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. In the final answer, list the two records alphabetically by returned name before the comparison.
prompt:
Complete the read-only support operations check in the supplied sandbox and create `case-audit.txt` from retrieved full records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `CASES.md`, then use Pi's genuine shell execution tool to run the local `./casectl` executable for every support-registry lookup; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Search separately for the exact case name “Damaged exhibit shipment” in “Museum Desk” and the exact case name “Duplicate membership charge” in “Billing Desk”. Your first case-data assistant action must start both searches concurrently as two separate direct `./casectl` processes, with no other case operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both search processes have returned successfully. Proceed only if each search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID; otherwise stop without guessing, do not retrieve either branch, and do not create the report. When both searches resolve, your immediately following case-data assistant action must start both full-record retrievals concurrently as two separate direct `./casectl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their corresponding searches, and wait for both results. Take every reported name, location, stable ID, status, and date only from those successful full-record retrievals.

This is a read-only audit. Do not update, cancel, or notify either case, and do not invoke any case-data operation beyond the two required searches and two eligible retrievals. Do not alter support data, the runtime database, the execution journal, or protected files. Do not expose unrelated records or any fields beyond those required in the deliverable.

`case-audit.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank, newline-terminated plain-text sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other content. Sort the two record sentences in ascending alphabetical order by the exact names returned by the full-record retrievals. Format each record sentence exactly as `<name> at <location> has stable ID <id>, status <status>, and date <date>.` The third sentence must begin exactly `Comparison: statuses match;` when the retrieved statuses are identical, or `Comparison: statuses differ;` when they differ. If the retrieved dates are identical, complete it with ` dates match.` Otherwise complete it with ` <earlier name> is earlier than <later name>.`, substituting the exact returned names according to the retrieved dates. Include no literal angle brackets. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied files unchanged and make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
